info.name: CodeArena LeetCode Features
info.description:
# CodeArena LeetCode-Style APIs

Focused collection for auth dashboard, Google login, leaderboard, playlists, and discussions.

## Quick start
1. Run **Login** or **Google Login**
2. Run **Get Dashboard** for full user dashboard data
3. Browse **Playlists** (try slug `interview-150`)
4. Test **Discussions** on any problem slug

# CodeArena API Collection

Production-ready Postman collection for the **CodeArena** LeetCode-style backend.

## Setup
1. Import this collection into Postman.
2. Set collection variables (defaults provided).
3. Run **Register** or **Login** — `accessToken` and `refreshToken` are saved automatically.
4. Use protected endpoints — Bearer auth uses `{{accessToken}}`.

## Base URL
Default: `http://localhost:3000/api`

## Problem identifiers
Use **MongoDB ObjectId** or **slug** for `problemId` (e.g. `sum-of-array-elements`).
Default slugs from dataset: EASY=`sum-of-array-elements`, MEDIUM=`find-pivot-index`, HARD=`target-sum-assign-signs`

## Code execution (C++ only)
- **Language:** C++ only — Judge0 `language_id: 54` (GCC 9.4.0)
- **Run** (`POST /submissions/run`) — judges **visible test cases only** (sync)
- **Submit** (`POST /submissions`) — judges **all test cases** via Redis worker (async)
- Execution service: CodeBox / Judge0 at `EXECUTION_API_URL` with `X-Auth-Token`
- Submit a **complete C++ program** with `main()` that reads stdin and prints stdout

## Recommended test flow
1. **Login** → saves tokens
2. **Get All Problems** → saves `problemId` + `problemSlug`
3. **Get Problem By Slug** → view hints + starter code
4. **Run Code** → test against public cases
5. **Submit Code** → full judge (start `npm run dev:worker`)
6. **Get Submission** → poll until not PENDING/RUNNING

## Auth Flow
1. `POST /auth/register` or `POST /auth/login`
2. Tokens saved to collection variables via test scripts
3. Protected routes use `Authorization: Bearer {{accessToken}}`

## Roles
- `USER` — submit code, view problems, profile
- `ADMIN` — manage problems and tags

## Response Format
```json
{ "success": true, "data": { ... } }
```

## Error Format
```json
{ "success": false, "message": "Error description" }
```
Validation errors (400) include an `errors` array with `field` and `message`.

item.name: 1. Authentication
item.description: User registration, login, token refresh, and session management.

request.description:
Register a new user account. Returns user object, access token, and refresh token.

**Validation:**
- username: 3-30 chars, alphanumeric + underscore
- email: valid email
- password: min 8 chars

response.name: 400 Bad Request

request.description: Authenticate with email and password. Automatically saves `accessToken` and `refreshToken` to collection variables.
Invalidate the refresh token. Pass the refresh token in the request body.
Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access/refresh token pair. Old refresh token is revoked.

Sign in with Google using an ID token from Google Sign-In on the frontend.

Requires `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` in server `.env`.
Get the authenticated user profile with solve stats. Requires valid Bearer access token.
Get public user profile with solve statistics and recent submissions.
Update authenticated user profile (avatar, username).
Change password for authenticated user.

response.name: 400 Bad Request

request.description: Get global leaderboard ranked by total problems solved. Pass Bearer token optionally to get `myRank`.

item.name: 7. Playlists
item.description: Official curated playlists and user-created problem lists.

request.description: List official playlists and public user playlists. Use `?official=true` for curated lists only.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Get playlist details with ordered problem list. Official slugs: `interview-150`, `master-array`, `dp-master`, `graph-essentials`, `blind-75-style`.
Create a user playlist. Anyone can create playlists.
Add a problem to your playlist by slug.
Delete your own playlist. Official playlists cannot be deleted.
List discussions for a problem by slug.
Get a discussion thread with all replies.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Start a new discussion on a problem.
Post a reply to a discussion.

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Toggle upvote on a discussion.

item.name: 9. Dashboard
item.description: LeetCode-style authenticated user dashboard — stats, progress rings, streak, submission calendar.

request.description:
Single endpoint for the auth dashboard UI.

Returns:
- **profile** — user info
- **stats** — total solved, rank, acceptance rate, streak
- **progress** — easy/medium/hard/overall rings (solved vs total)
- **recentAccepted** — last 5 solved problems
- **recentSubmissions** — last 10 submissions
- **submissionCalendar** — heatmap data `{ "YYYY-MM-DD": count }`

response.name: 200 OK
